title: Ironhump Freight Yard: Single-Hump Shunting Priority
strategies: first-come-first-served dispatch: hump the cuts strictly in arrival (release) order, ignoring weights and cut-offs (reproduces the evaluator's FCFS reference, ~0.1)

static single-machine dispatch rules: prioritize by weighted-shortest-processing-time (Smith's ratio w/p) or earliest-due-date (EDD), which slashes weighted lateness versus FCFS but ignores the release staggering
look-ahead dispatch heuristics: Apparent-Tardiness-Cost (ATC) style priorities that blend w/p with a slack/exp discount so heavy urgent cuts jump the queue near their cut-off

seeded local search / metaheuristics: start from the best classic rule and relocate/swap cuts (insertion or 2-opt neighbourhood, perturb-and-restart) scored on the same non-delay simulator to reach a strong local optimum of 1|r_j|sum w_j T_j
